kaggle_note: CSV files under data/benchmark/kaggle/ come from public Kaggle datasets. Run: python scripts/download_kaggle_benchmark_data.py. See benchmarks/kaggle_sources.json for slugs and URLs.

datasets.ml_schema_hint: Sample sales (classic model DB): ORDERNUMBER, QUANTITYORDERED, PRICEEACH, ORDERLINENUMBER, SALES, ORDERDATE, STATUS, QTR_ID, MONTH_ID, YEAR_ID, PRODUCTLINE, MSRP, PRODUCTCODE, CUSTOMERNAME, PHONE, ADDRESSLINE1, ADDRESSLINE2, CITY, STATE, POSTALCODE, COUNTRY, TERRITORY, CONTACTLASTNAME, CONTACTFIRSTNAME, DEALSIZE.

tasks.task: Load the CSV and report row count, column count, and list all column names.
Find the top 3 PRODUCTLINE values by row count and plot a vertical bar chart.
tasks.expected_output: Bar chart of top product lines

tasks.task: Compute mean SALES and mean PRICEEACH across all rows.
Plot a histogram of QUANTITYORDERED.
Compare average SALES for each DEALSIZE category (Small, Medium, Large if present).
Scatter plot of PRICEEACH vs SALES for rows where PRODUCTLINE is Classic Cars.
Report percentage of missing values in SALES and in PRODUCTLINE.
Which COUNTRY has the highest total SALES? Show a sorted table and one chart.
Compare mean MSRP by PRODUCTLINE for the top 5 product lines by volume.
Count rows where STATUS equals Shipped.
Using a train/test split, predict SALES from numeric columns QUANTITYORDERED, PRICEEACH, MSRP, QTR_ID, MONTH_ID, YEAR_ID; report R2 or RMSE and note strongest drivers in words.
Predict DEALSIZE (treat as categorical target) from numeric features available; report accuracy or macro-F1.

Using only cross-sectional fields in this file, identify which PRODUCTLINE has the highest predicted total next-month demand proxy (explain that true forecasting needs dates; use sum of SALES by line as baseline and optionally fit a simple rank model).

Dual-method reconciliation audit: compute total SALES by PRODUCTLINE in two independent ways (A: pandas groupby and B: SQL query via sqlite3 in-memory table). Output both result tables sorted identically, then produce a discrepancy report with max absolute difference, number of mismatched groups, and a root-cause note if differences exist. Save one bar chart of reconciled totals and its JSON sidecar.
tasks.expected_output: Two independent KPI computations, discrepancy report, and reconciled chart
tasks.reference_metrics_hint: Fail if either method is missing, if reconciliation metrics are missing, or if discrepancies are not explicitly discussed.

tasks.task: Leakage-controlled forecasting protocol: parse ORDERDATE, build a time-ordered split (earliest 80% train, latest 20% test), and predict SALES using numeric features only. Train a baseline linear model and one stronger nonlinear model, report RMSE and MAE for both on the same holdout, and include a leakage checklist stating which columns were excluded to avoid target leakage. End with one paragraph on limitations and uncertainty.
tasks.expected_output: Time-split baseline vs advanced model with leakage checklist and uncertainty note
tasks.reference_metrics_hint: Require explicit time-based split evidence, two models with comparable metrics, leakage discussion, and limitation statement.

tasks.task: Reviewer-gated visualization package: deliver exactly three business-relevant charts (trend, segment comparison, and distribution), each saved as PNG with required JSON sidecar schema. Include a final QA checklist confirming for each chart: title clarity, axis labels, value arrays present in sidecar, and one-sentence business interpretation. If any chart fails the checklist, revise before final answer.
tasks.expected_output: Three validated charts with complete sidecars and QA checklist
tasks.reference_metrics_hint: Fail if fewer than three charts, missing/invalid sidecar fields, or no explicit QA checklist pass/fix evidence.

datasets.ml_schema_hint: Stroke prediction: id, gender, age, hypertension, heart_disease, ever_married, work_type, Residence_type, avg_glucose_level, bmi, smoking_status, stroke (0/1 target).

tasks.task: Summarize dataset shape and dtypes.
Count rows by gender and plot a bar chart.
Mean and median age overall.
Histogram of avg_glucose_level.
Average age for stroke=1 vs stroke=0.
Scatter bmi vs age (drop or impute missing bmi for plotting).
Stroke rate (mean of stroke) by work_type.
Share of patients with stroke equal to 1.
Box plot of avg_glucose_level by smoking_status.
Correlation between age and avg_glucose_level.
Predict stroke from age, hypertension, heart_disease, avg_glucose_level, bmi (handle missing bmi sensibly) with a train/test split; report accuracy and note class imbalance.
Cluster rows into 3 groups using standardized numeric columns age, avg_glucose_level, bmi (impute missing bmi); briefly interpret clusters.
Which numeric features appear most associated with stroke? Use a tree-based model or correlation-based ranking and summarize.

datasets.ml_schema_hint: Bank marketing (UCI): age, job, marital, education, default, housing, loan, contact, month, day_of_week, duration, campaign, pdays, previous, poutcome, emp.var.rate, cons.price.idx, cons.conf.idx, euribor3m, nr.employed, y (yes/no subscription).

tasks.task: Report number of rows and columns.
Subscription rate: proportion where y is yes.
Histogram of age.
Bar chart of counts by job.
Mean duration for y=yes vs y=no.
Mean campaign for y=yes vs y=no.
Box plot of age grouped by y.
Top 5 marital categories by count (table).
Correlation matrix of numeric columns age, duration, campaign, pdays, previous, emp.var.rate, cons.price.idx, cons.conf.idx, euribor3m, nr.employed.
Compare mean euribor3m for y=yes vs y=no.
Train a classifier to predict y from age, duration, campaign, pdays, previous and the macro-economic numeric columns; use stratified train/test split; report accuracy and mention imbalance.
Train a model to predict duration from age, campaign, euribor3m, nr.employed; report R2 on a holdout set.
Use an anomaly-detection approach on numeric call-center fields (duration, campaign, pdays) and list three unusual row indices or ages worth reviewing.

datasets.ml_schema_hint: Student math (UCI): school, sex, age, address, famsize, Pstatus, Medu, Fedu, Mjob, Fjob, reason, guardian, traveltime, studytime, failures, schoolsup, famsup, paid, activities, nursery, higher, internet, romantic, famrel, freetime, goout, Dalc, Walc, health, absences, G1, G2, G3.

tasks.task: Dataset shape and column list.
Bar chart of student counts by school.
Mean final grade G3 by sex.
Histogram of studytime.
Mean Walc (weekend alcohol) by romantic yes vs no.
Scatter G1 vs G3 colored or grouped by a simple binary column of your choice.
Top reason value by count.
Median absences by failures (table).
Correlation between goout and G3.
Mean G3 for students with failures=0 vs failures>0.
Predict G3 from studytime, failures, absences, G1, G2 with train/test split; report RMSE or R2.
Predict higher (yes/no) from numeric studytime, failures, freetime, goout, health; report accuracy.
Cluster students on standardized numeric lifestyle fields (goout, Dalc, Walc, freetime) into 3 clusters and summarize profiles.

datasets.ml_schema_hint: Bike sharing daily: instant, dteday, season, yr, mnth, holiday, weekday, workingday, weathersit, temp, atemp, hum, windspeed, casual, registered, cnt.

tasks.task: Report shape and list columns.
Bar chart of mean cnt by season.
Histogram of windspeed.
Scatter temp vs cnt.
Mean registered rides by workingday (0 vs 1).
Total cnt by weathersit (table).
Line or bar of cnt over dteday for the first 60 rows (time series style).
Mean hum by season.
Top 5 days by cnt (show dteday and cnt).
Correlation between casual and registered.
Predict cnt from temp, atemp, hum, windspeed, season, yr, mnth, holiday, workingday, weathersit using train/test split; report R2 or RMSE.
Classify high-demand days (cnt above median) using numeric weather and calendar features; report accuracy.
Detect outlier days using isolation-style logic on temp, hum, windspeed, cnt; list three dteday values that stand out.
